expectations.text: Spec has all required sections including Acceptance Criteria
expectations.evidence: All 9 required headings present: ## Problem (L12), ## Solution (L17), ## Scope (L20), ## Acceptance Criteria (L41), ## Design Decisions (L51), ### Failure Modes (L67), ## Risks & Open Questions (L74), ## Alternatives Considered (L80), ## Verification (L86).

expectations.text: No code fences in the spec outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Scanned all 124 lines. Zero triple-backtick (```) occurrences anywhere in the spec. Inline backticks exist (e.g. `pnpm run build` at L97, `#dash-annotations` at L45) but those are not code fences.

expectations.text: No TypeScript interface/type definitions in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions section (L51-L72) contains no 'interface', 'type' keyword in TS context, ': string', ': number', or any TypeScript syntax. All descriptions are prose.

expectations.text: No proto message definitions or field listings in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions describes annotation data in prose: 'Fields capture start time, end time, display text, an optional category tag, creator identity, and a stable identifier for URL linking' (L55). No proto message syntax, no field-name-and-type listings, no schema definitions.

expectations.text: Scope items are capabilities, not file paths
expectations.evidence: In Scope (L24-L31) contains 8 items. None contain exact file paths or 'NEW: src/...' entries. Items reference capabilities: CRUD operations, rendering behavior, panel context menu, tab, URL parameters, chat tools, proto/API (architectural, not file paths).

expectations.text: Spec is under 120 lines excluding Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Total spec: 124 lines. Implementation Delta (L116-L119) and AGENTS.md Updates (L121-L124) are post-spec boilerplate sections. Excluding L116-L124 (9 lines): 115 lines of spec content. Under max_lines=120.

expectations.text: Design Decisions use bold topic sentences
expectations.evidence: 7/7 Design Decisions paragraphs open with bold: L53 '**Annotations are a server-persisted resource...**', L55 '**A new proto message type...**', L57 '**Time-series panel rendering...**', L59 '**The panel context menu...**', L61 '**URL parameters encode...**', L63 '**The Annotations tab...**', L65 '**Chat integration follows...**'. 100% > 80% threshold.

expectations.text: Implementer could start without asking questions
expectations.evidence: All 6 required areas covered: annotation data model (L53-55: dashboard-scoped, fields enumerated), rendering approach (L57-58: shared visual layer, clipping, label positioning), URL param selection (L61-62: stable ID, highlight styling), chat tool behaviors (L65-66: three tools with inputs/outputs/conventions), panel context menu (L59-60: seeds with visible range, existing pattern), annotations tab (L63-64: tab hash, filterable list, entry contents).

expectations.text: Design Decisions contains decisions not implementation
expectations.evidence: FAIL — React hook implementation details in Design Decisions. L65: names specific hooks `useDashboardAnnotationTools` and `useLatestRef`, references specific type `ChatTool`. L57: names specific component `PanelGrid`. These are implementation-level names, not design-level decisions. The assertion explicitly lists 'React hook implementation details' as a failure criterion.

3/4 (75%) are genuine policy decisions teams could disagree on: (1) L69 clip-to-bounds with truncation indicator vs. hide partial vs. auto-expand — debatable policy; (2) L71 toast + render normally on missing annotation vs. modal/redirect/placeholder — debatable policy; (3) L72 cap visible annotations with 'N more hidden' vs. render all/zoom/density controls — debatable policy. L70 (reject invalid time range) is standard validation, not policy. 75% > 50%.

expectations.text: Scope items describe behaviors not files
expectations.evidence: 7/8 In Scope items (87.5%) are behavioral capabilities: L24 CRUD operations, L26 rendering as shaded regions, L27 panel context menu creation, L28 annotations tab with filtering, L29 URL parameter sharing, L30 chat tools, L25 data capture (user-facing fields). 1 non-behavioral: L31 'New proto message type... and CRUD API endpoint' is an implementation artifact. 87.5% > 80%.

expectations.text: Spec has well-formed Acceptance Criteria with behavioral outcomes
expectations.evidence: ## Acceptance Criteria at L41. 7 checkbox items (L43-L49), within 4-8 range. All behavioral/observable: panel rendering (L43), context menu creation (L44), tab listing+filtering (L45), URL-param highlighting (L46), chat create (L47), chat list (L48), deletion (L49). Covers all core features: creation from menu (L44) + chat (L47), visibility on panels (L43), URL sharing (L46), tab with filtering (L45), chat behaviors (L47-L48).

### Agent-Verifiable subsection at L103. 6 checks (L104-L109), all in action → expected outcome format: 'Create annotation via API → 201 response' (L104), 'GET filtered by dashboard ID → only matching annotations' (L105), 'Load with valid annotation param → highlight styling applied' (L106), 'Load with deleted annotation param → no crash, toast rendered' (L107), 'Inspect PanelGrid → overlay elements in TIMESERIES/HEATMAP only' (L108), 'Invoke chat tool → annotation persists in tab' (L109). Concrete and agent-executable.
